summary: The post argues that VCs contain two competing personas: a sincere helper who loves collaborating with founders and a harsher, status‑driven operator who wants to be feared and seen as decisive. While both modes can be useful, the author stresses that performative toughness and insecurity are easy to spot, and authenticity matters more than cosplay.
money_quotes: Inside every venture capitalist there are two wolves.
The second wolf will shiv you for a dollar.
Inside every venture capitalist, there are both wolves.
Nobody is as good of an actor as they think they are.
You can tell when someone is desperate to prove that they’re useful and not just rich.
key_insight: VCs oscillate between helpful collaboration and performative toughness, but the insecurity behind the latter is transparent and damaging.
